design.title: ScreenWise — AI Fitment Copilot for Recruiters
design.style_name: Clean product pitch — emerald/slate/amber, Inter + JetBrains Mono
design.reference: Linear / Notion / Stripe dashboard aesthetic — clean, product-forward, lots of whitespace. NOT flashy. The assignment explicitly values execution over polish.

slides.title: Problem Understanding
slides.task_brief:
SLIDE 1 of 6 — Part 1: Problem Understanding (max 1 slide per the assignment).

Eyebrow pill (top-left): 'PART 1 · PROBLEM UNDERSTANDING'

Main headline (h-display, 2 lines, max 9 words per line):
'Recruiters are drowning in resumes — and missing great candidates.'

Subhead (one line, body, muted): 'For every open role, ~200 applicants. ~75% are unqualified — but finding the qualified 25% means reading every single line.'

Layout: headline + subhead at top (occupies top ~38% of slide). Below it, a 2x2 grid of 4 small cards answering the 4 questions from the assignment. Each card has a tiny eyebrow label (uppercase, micro, emerald), a short title (h3), and 2-3 lines of body text. Be concise — each card ~25-35 words.

Card 1 — label 'THE PROBLEM':
Title: 'Volume vs. bandwidth'
Body: '200 resumes per role. ~90 seconds to read each one. By resume #40, judgment blurs — and the strongest candidate might be #180.'

Card 2 — label 'WHO IT HURTS':
Title: 'In-house recruiters & hiring managers'
Body: 'Especially GCC talent-acquisition teams hiring at volume across multiple roles. The ones who care most about quality are also the most time-starved.'

Card 3 — label 'WHY IT MATTERS':
Title: 'Time-to-hire = revenue'
Body: 'Every unfilled engineering role costs ~$7K/day in lost output. Burnout is at record highs. And the best candidates get missed — not because they weren\'t qualified, but because no one had bandwidth to read them carefully.'

Card 4 — label 'WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE':
Title: 'Minutes, not hours'
Body: 'Cut first-pass screening from hours to minutes per role. Surface the top 10-15% with explainable reasoning. Free recruiters to actually talk to candidates instead of reading about them.'

At the very bottom of the content area (above the slide footer), a single italic line in muted small text, in quotes, signed: '"I picked this problem because I\'ve watched friends on both sides — recruiters exhausted by resume piles, and strong candidates who never got a fair first read."'

Slide footer: brand 'ScreenWise · Embark Assignment' on the left, '01 / 06' on the right.

No images. Pure typographic layout. Use the .eyebrow, .h-display, .h3, .body, .card classes from global.css. The 2x2 grid should have ~16px gaps and each card should feel airy (not cramped). Use the emerald accent sparingly — only on the eyebrow, card labels, and bullet chips if any.

slides.title: Solution — Workflow & Features
slides.layout: split: left workflow steps, right feature list
slides.task_brief:
SLIDE 2 of 6 — Part 2: Product Solution, slide 1 of 2.

Eyebrow pill (top-left): 'PART 2 · PRODUCT SOLUTION (1/2)'

Main headline (h1, single line): 'ScreenWise — paste a JD and a batch of resumes, get a ranked shortlist in under a minute.'

Subhead (body, muted, one line): 'No logins, no setup. The recruiter stays in control — ScreenWise recommends, never auto-rejects.'

Layout: two columns side by side, ~55% left / ~45% right, with a 28px gap.

LEFT COLUMN — 'Product workflow' (h3 heading, then 4 numbered steps stacked vertically). Each step has a .chip-num circle on the left, then a one-line bold title and a one-line body description. Steps:
1. 'Paste JD + resumes' — 'Recruiter pastes the job description and a batch of resumes (separated by --- on their own line). No file upload needed for the prototype.'
2. 'AI parses the JD' — 'Extracts must-haves, nice-to-haves, seniority signals, and domain context into a structured brief.'
3. 'Score on a 5-signal rubric' — 'Each candidate gets a 0-100 fitment score with per-signal reasoning (skills, experience, seniority, domain, trajectory).'
4. 'Ranked dashboard + outreach' — 'Recruiter sees a ranked list with red flags, skill gaps, and a personalized outreach email per candidate — ready to copy.'

RIGHT COLUMN — 'Key features' (h3 heading, then 5 compact feature cards stacked vertically, each ~50px tall). Each feature card has a small icon-style square (use a Unicode glyph or simple shape, emerald background, white glyph) on the left, then a bold short title and a one-line body. Features:
• Rubric — '5 calibrated signals, weighted: Skills 40 · Experience 25 · Seniority 15 · Domain 10 · Trajectory 10.'
• Explainable — 'Every score comes with matched skills, missing skills, strengths, and red flags. No black boxes.'
• Red-flag surfacing — 'Job-hopping, scope mismatch, gaps — surfaced for the recruiter, never used to auto-reject.'
• Personalized outreach — 'One-click email draft that references a SPECIFIC detail from that candidate\'s resume.'
• Bias guardrails — 'Name-blind scoring option. Strong Fit (≥80) is intentionally rare — most candidates land in Possible or Pass.'

For the feature glyphs, use simple Material Icons via the icon font: <list_checks>, <eye>, <flag>, <mail>, <shield>. Load Material Icons in <head>.

Slide footer: brand 'ScreenWise · Embark Assignment' left, '02 / 06' right.

No photos. Use .card, .chip-num, .h3, .body, .pill-soft-emerald classes. Keep vertical rhythm tight — this slide is dense, so use 12-14px gaps between items, not 20px.

slides.title: Solution — Journey, Assumptions, Impact
slides.task_brief:
SLIDE 3 of 6 — Part 2: Product Solution, slide 2 of 2.

Eyebrow pill (top-left): 'PART 2 · PRODUCT SOLUTION (2/2)'

Main headline (h1, single line): 'A recruiter\'s Tuesday afternoon, before and after ScreenWise.'

Layout: 3 stacked sections.

SECTION A — 'User journey: before vs. after' (h3 heading). Two side-by-side cards. Each card is full-width-half with a colored top border (BEFORE = slate-300 top border; AFTER = emerald top border). Each card has a small label at top ('BEFORE' in slate, 'AFTER' in emerald), a one-line scenario title, then 4 short bullet steps. Use .bullets-slate for BEFORE and .bullets for AFTER.

BEFORE card title: 'Manual screening, 5 hours per role'
BEFORE bullets:
• Open 200-resume ATS folder, read each (~90s each = ~5 hours)
• Flag ~30 maybes based on gut feel
• Sync with hiring manager to debate the shortlist
• Manually draft outreach to 10 candidates — generic templates

AFTER card title: 'ScreenWise-assisted, 45 minutes per role'
AFTER bullets:
• Paste JD + 200 resumes, click Run
• 60 seconds later: ranked shortlist of top 15, with explanations
• Review red flags and strengths, adjust shortlist in one click
• Copy personalized outreach drafts, send from own inbox

SECTION B — 'Assumptions' (h3 heading) + 'Expected impact' (h3 heading). Side by side, 50/50.

ASSUMPTIONS card (.card-soft, amber accent on the label): 4 short bullets, each one line:
• Resumes arrive as text (paste or ATS export). PDF parsing is a v1.1 problem.
• One recruiter owns one role at a time. Multi-recruiter collaboration is post-MVP.
• The JD has at least a paragraph of real content. Two lines won\'t help anyone.
• Outreach goes through the recruiter\'s inbox — ScreenWise drafts, never sends.

EXPECTED IMPACT card (.card-emerald): 4 stat lines, each with a big number (font-num, 22px, emerald) and a short label (small, muted). Stack vertically with 10px gaps:
• '~85%' — 'reduction in first-pass screening time per role'
• '4.5 hrs' — 'saved per role, per recruiter, per week'
• 'Higher' — 'response rates from personalized outreach vs. templates'
• '100%' — 'of shortlist decisions come with a written rationale'

Slide footer: brand left, '03 / 06' right.

No photos. Dense slide — use 14px gaps between sections, 10px within cards. Use Material Icons for the section heading decorations: <schedule> for journey, <help_outline> for assumptions, <trending_up> for impact.

slides.title: AI Approach
slides.layout: model choice + how AI is used + reliability ladder
slides.task_brief:
SLIDE 4 of 6 — Part 3: AI Approach (max 1 slide).

Eyebrow pill (top-left): 'PART 3 · AI APPROACH'

Main headline (h1, single line): 'A calibrated LLM evaluator with structured output and a human in the loop.'

Subhead (body, muted): 'One model does the whole pipeline. The hard part isn\'t picking a model — it\'s writing the rubric and forcing the model to be honest.'

Layout: 3 columns side by side, equal width, 20px gaps. Each column is a tall card with a colored top accent strip.

COLUMN 1 — top strip emerald. Heading (h3): 'Model & tools'
Body content (use .bullets):
• GPT-4-class LLM via z-ai-web-dev-sdk — one structured call per batch
• Next.js 16 API route server-side — SDK never touches the browser
• JSON schema output — no free-text parsing, ever
• No embeddings or classifiers for v0.1 — the LLM reasons about BOTH 'does this candidate have AWS ECS?' AND 'is 4 jobs in 4 years a red flag?' in a single pass. Stitching 4 specialized models would be over-engineering for a prototype.

COLUMN 2 — top strip amber. Heading (h3): 'How AI is used'
Body content (4 mini-rows, each with a small left arrow glyph '→' in emerald, then a one-liner):
• JD → structured must-haves, nice-to-haves, seniority, domain
• Resume → normalized profile (skills, scope, trajectory)
• Score → 0-100 across 5 weighted signals, with per-candidate reasoning
• Outreach → 2-paragraph email referencing a SPECIFIC resume detail (project, metric, role)

COLUMN 3 — top strip slate. Heading (h3): 'Quality & reliability'
Body content (use .bullets-slate, tighter):
• Explicit rubric in the prompt — the model isn\'t guessing what 'good' means
• Structured JSON schema — parsing failures are caught, not silent
• Calibrated thresholds — Strong Fit (≥80) is intentionally rare. Most candidates land in Possible (60-79) or Pass (<60)
• Bias guardrails in system prompt — judge on skills, scope, trajectory ONLY; never on name, gender, age, or university prestige
• Human-in-the-loop — ScreenWise recommends, the recruiter decides. No auto-reject.
• Future: dual-pass scoring on borderline calls; embeddings for semantic skill matching at scale (so 'TS' matches 'TypeScript')

At the bottom of the slide (above footer), one italic muted line in quotes: '"The first version of the prompt gave almost everyone 75+. The fix wasn\'t a bigger model — it was telling the model that Strong Fit should be rare."'

Slide footer: brand left, '04 / 06' right.

No photos. Use .card with a colored top strip (4px height). Material Icons for column headers: <memory>, <auto_awesome>, <verified>.

slides.title: Prototype
slides.task_brief: SLIDE 5 of 6 — Part 4: Prototype. Eyebrow pill (top-left): 'PART 4 · PROTOTYPE' Main headline (h1, single line): 'Built in ~5 hours. End-to-end working. Try it live.' Subhead (body, muted): 'I kept the UI utilitarian on purpose — the assignment said it values execution over polish, so I spent the hours on AI calibration, not animations.' Layout: two columns, ~58% left (screenshot) / ~42% right (callouts + stack + links), 24px gap. LEFT COLUMN — A framed screenshot of the prototype. Use this local image with a RELATIVE path: . Above the image, a tiny label 'LIVE PROTOTYPE — RANKED RESULTS' in micro uppercase muted. Below the image, one line of small muted text: 'Sample run: Senior Backend Engineer JD (fintech) + 5 candidate resumes. AI correctly flagged the 6-yr Node.js+Postgres+fintech engineer as Strong Fit, and surfaced the 4-jobs-in-4-years pattern as a red flag on another.' RIGHT COLUMN — Three stacked sections. Section 1 — 'What it does' (h3 + 4 short bullets): • Paste a JD + paste resumes (one per block, separated by ---) • Click 'Run AI fitment analysis' • Get ranked cards: score, recommendation, matched/missing skills, key strengths, red flags, one-line summary, personalized outreach email • Copy outreach email in one click, mark Shortlist or Pass per candidate Section 2 — 'Stack' (h3 + one-line tags row using .pill-soft-slate pills): Next.js 16 · TypeScript · Tailwind · shadcn/ui · z-ai-web-dev-sdk (server-side) · No database (stateless prototype) Section 3 — 'Try it' (h3 + 2 link rows). Use .card-emerald for this block. Each link row: a small icon + label + URL in monospace. • Live prototype: [preview URL provided after deployment] • GitHub repo: [repo URL] If you need a placeholder for the URLs, write 'preview URL' and 'github.com/your-handle/screenwise' — keep them in monospace, emerald color, underlined. Slide footer: brand left, '05 / 06' right. The screenshot image MUST use the relative path 'prototype-results.png' (it lives in the same directory as this HTML file). Do NOT use an absolute path. Do NOT use a remote URL.

slides.title: Reflection
slides.task_brief:
SLIDE 6 of 6 — Part 5: Reflection (max 1 slide).

Eyebrow pill (top-left): 'PART 5 · REFLECTION'

Main headline (h1, single line): 'What I learned shipping this in 5 hours.'

Layout: 2x2 grid of 4 reflection cards (each ~280px wide, ~220px tall), with a final full-width personal note bar at the bottom.

Card 1 (top-left, .card) — label 'AI TOOLS I USED' (micro, emerald, uppercase):
Title (h3): 'The stack behind the stack'
Body (small): 'Cursor + Claude for scaffolding the Next.js app and shadcn/ui components. GPT-4-class model (via z-ai-web-dev-sdk) as the actual fitment engine inside the prototype. I did NOT use AI to write the rubric or the bias guardrails — those came from reading about real recruiter workflows first.'

Card 2 (top-right, .card) — label 'HOW AI HELPED' (micro, emerald, uppercase):
Title (h3): '10x on the boring parts'
Body (small): 'API routes, type definitions, component scaffolding — all 10x faster. It also caught edge cases I would have missed (empty resumes, oversized batches). That freed my time for the parts that actually matter: rubric design and prompt calibration.'

Card 3 (bottom-left, .card-amber) — label 'BIGGEST CHALLENGE' (micro, amber, uppercase, color #92400e):
Title (h3): 'Calibration, not capability'
Body (small): 'The first prompt gave almost everyone 75+. The model wasn\'t dumb — it was being too polite. Fix: explicit rubric weights, a hard rule that Strong Fit (≥80) should be rare, and a calibration line in the system prompt. V2 correctly flagged only 1 of 5 sample candidates as Strong Fit.'

Card 4 (bottom-right, .card-emerald) — label 'IF I HAD ANOTHER WEEK' (micro, emerald, uppercase):
Title (h3): 'Where I\'d take it next'
Body (small, use .bullets style with 4 very short items):
• Bias-audit dashboard (track score distributions across name/gender proxies)
• Real PDF resume parsing, not just paste
• Embeddings-based semantic skill matching ('TS' → 'TypeScript')
• Greenhouse / Lever integration + a second-opinion pass on borderline calls

PERSONAL NOTE BAR — full width at bottom, above the slide footer. A .card-soft with a left emerald border (4px solid emerald). Italic body text in ink-2 color, in quotes:
'"This was the most fun I\'ve had on a take-home. The hardest part wasn\'t the code — it was resisting the urge to over-build. I kept asking: would a recruiter actually use this on Monday?"'

Slide footer: brand 'ScreenWise · Embark Assignment' left, '06 / 06' right. Add a tiny thank-you in muted micro next to the page number: 'Thank you — looking forward to the conversation.'

No photos. Use Material Icons for the 4 card labels: <build>, <bolt>, <warning_amber>, <rocket_launch>. Keep the 2x2 grid airy with 16px gaps.
